name: Reddit, Inc.
one_liner: Operates a user-generated discussion-and-voting platform organised into thousands of topic communities (subreddits); monetises via ads and AI-data licensing.
business_model: Two revenue engines. (1) Advertising â€” promoted posts, banner ads, and sponsored content sold against the platform's 121M daily active users. (2) Data licensing â€” recurring fees from AI labs (Google, OpenAI) for training-data access to Reddit's 25B+ posts and comments, plus a small but high-margin Reddit Premium subscription tier.
products_services: Reddit web platform and iOS/Android apps
Reddit Answers â€” AI search tool launched Dec 2024
Data licensing API for AI training (Google, OpenAI)
customers: Advertisers (mostly US brands, scaling internationally) and AI/ML companies that license bulk training data; consumer side is free.
revenue_geography: Predominantly US-driven advertising; data-licensing revenue concentrated in two large US AI customers (Google, OpenAI).

competitors.name: X (formerly Twitter)
competitors.note: rival public-conversation network; weaker community structure but larger ad spend

competitors.name: Meta (Facebook Groups, Threads)
competitors.note: incumbent ad giant with overlapping community-discussion features

competitors.name: TikTok
competitors.note: competes for attention and ad budget, especially among Gen Z

moat: 20+ years of accumulated, structured human conversation (25B+ posts/comments) â€” a corpus AI labs increasingly need and cannot replicate. Strong community network effects and topic-specific subreddit lock-in.

key_people.name: Steve Huffman
key_people.background: Co-founded Reddit 2005; returned as CEO 2015 after stints at Hipmunk.

key_people.name: Alexis Ohanian
key_people.background: Co-founded Reddit 2005; no longer in operating role but remains influential public figure.

notable_investors: Advance Publications (~30%, Newhouse family â€” pre-IPO controlling shareholder)
bull_points: Seven consecutive quarters of 60%+ revenue growth; Q1 2026 revenue $663M, +69% YoY
91.5% gross margin and 40% adjusted EBITDA margin â€” high software-like profitability
Unique 25B-post training-data corpus is a structural moat as AI demand scales
Existing Google + OpenAI licensing deals (~$50â€“60M/yr each) up for renewal 2027 â€” likely repriced higher
Reddit Answers AI feature opens a new search-revenue surface within the app
bear_points: Stock down ~37% YTD 2026 â€” sentiment cooled despite earnings beats
Advertising still ~85% of revenue; cyclical exposure to digital-ad budgets
Data-licensing revenue concentrated in two customers (Google, OpenAI) â€” renewal risk
Heavy reliance on unpaid volunteer moderators â€” fragile content-quality model
Brand-safety perception (controversial subreddits, misinformation) limits some advertisers
red_flags: 2023 API pricing change triggered the largest user/moderator protest in Reddit history; risk of repeat with future monetisation moves
FTC opened inquiry into AI data-licensing practices ahead of IPO (March 2024)
Concentration of voting power post-IPO with founders + Advance Publications dual-class structure
catalysts: 2027 renewals of Google and OpenAI data-licensing contracts â€” CEO publicly hinting at higher pricing
Potential new AI-licensing deals (Anthropic, Meta, xAI)

recent_news.headline: Reddit Q1 2026: revenue +69% to $663M, EBITDA margin 40%, net income $204M
Reddit CEO Huffman warns Google and OpenAI: 'no AI without actual intelligence' â€” flags 2027 renewal pricing
RDDT trades $84 below average price target after 37% YTD 2026 decline
Reddit revenue beat clouded by AI-data concerns and regulatory scrutiny
Reddit prices IPO at $34/share, opens at $47, valuing it at ~$9.5B
